attributes.description: Alerts when task instance failures exceed 10 in the evaluation window (`airflow.ti_failures > 10`). Elevated task failures usually reflect application bugs or unstable dependencies.
attributes.name: [Airflow OTel] Task failures above threshold

investigation_guide.blob:
## Airflow task failures above threshold

### What fired
Summed `airflow.ti_failures` over the evaluation window exceeded 10.

### Why it matters
A spike in task failures degrades DAG completion rates and burns through retry budgets, amplifying load on downstream systems. Failures are usually concentrated on a handful of DAGs or operators.

### Triage
1. Tasks dashboard: identify which DAGs/tasks are failing most.
2. Sample a failing task log for the root cause (application error, dependency timeout, OOM).
3. Correlate with infrastructure events: deployments, DB issues, third-party outages.

### Remediation
- Fix the failing task or its upstream dependency.
- If a dependency is flaky, add retries with exponential backoff — but alert if retry counts are the dominant driver of load.

### Tuning
- 10 failures / 15 min is mid-range. Lower for SLA-critical DAGs; raise for large noisy pipelines.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-airflow.otel-default*
| WHERE data_stream.dataset == "airflow.otel"
| WHERE airflow.ti_failures IS NOT NULL
// Sum task instance failures in the time window
| STATS failure_count = SUM(airflow.ti_failures)
// Adjust threshold (10) for your expected failure volume
| WHERE failure_count > 10
